quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: June 1st marks the beginning of meteorological summer in the Northern Hemisphere! This is when scientists officially start counting the summer season, based on temperature patterns rather than the astronomical solstice. It's a wonderful time to enjoy the long daylight hours on the Olympic Peninsula.

short_read.title: The Secret Lives of Harbor Seals
short_read.teaser: Harbor seals are frequent visitors to the Strait of Juan de Fuca, and they're more fascinating than you might think. Learn about their surprising diving abilities and how they raise their adorable pups.

medium_read.title: The Elwha River Restoration: Nature's Remarkable Comeback
medium_read.teaser: Right in Port Angeles's backyard, the Elwha River is showing us how nature can heal when given the chance. After the largest dam removal project in U.S. history, salmon are returning and the ecosystem is thriving in ways that have amazed scientists.

long_read.title: Hungary's Lake Balaton: The Heart of Hungarian Summer
long_read.teaser: Lake Balaton has been the beloved vacation destination for Hungarian families for generations, earning its nickname as the "Hungarian Sea." This beautiful exploration covers the lake's history, charming villages, and the traditions that make it special to Hungarians everywhere.

port_angeles.title: Black-Tailed Deer Fawning Season
port_angeles.description: Early June is when Olympic black-tailed deer give birth to their spotted fawns in the forests around Port Angeles. If you spot a fawn lying alone in the grass, don't worry — mother deer leave their babies hidden while they browse for food, returning several times a day to nurse. The best thing to do is admire from a distance and let nature take its course.

puzzle_1.question: An adult harbor seal can hold its breath underwater for up to ___ minutes while diving for fish.
puzzle_1.correct_response: Exactly right, Barbara! That's an impressive ability — longer than most people could imagine!
puzzle_1.close_response: You're very close! Harbor seals can actually hold their breath for about 25 minutes. That's remarkable!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: Harbor seals are amazing divers — they can hold their breath for up to 25 minutes! That helps them catch fish in the deep waters of the Strait of Juan de Fuca.
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: Harbor seals you might see from Port Angeles can dive as deep as 1,500 feet, though they usually stay in shallower waters. Their large eyes help them see in the murky depths where they hunt for fish, squid, and crustaceans.

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'flower'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Wonderful, Barbara! Virág is such a beautiful word for something so beautiful!
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The Hungarian word for flower is 'virág' — such a lovely word! (Madár means bird, napfény means sunshine, and kert means garden.)
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: Virág is also a common Hungarian surname and given name, reflecting the Hungarian love of nature and flowers. Many Hungarian words related to nature have a musical, poetic quality that makes the language especially beautiful.

puzzle_3.question: What type of tree is most common in Olympic National Park's temperate rainforest?
puzzle_3.correct_response: Perfect! The majestic Sitka spruce thrives in the misty rainforests near Port Angeles, some growing over 300 feet tall!
puzzle_3.wrong_response: It's the Sitka spruce! These magnificent trees love the moist, mild climate of the Olympic Peninsula and can grow to amazing heights, creating those magical misty forests we treasure here.
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: The Hoh Rain Forest and Quinault Rain Forest in Olympic National Park are home to some of the largest Sitka spruces in the world. These ancient trees create a cathedral-like canopy draped with mosses and ferns, receiving over 12 feet of rain each year.

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
